finding: Compatibility policy is not operational until every append-only queue-update artifact is discovered, parsed, classified, and allowed to block reconciliation fail-closed.

observed: The canonical queue is stale relative to append-only queue-update records.
Four deterministic Node.js tests passed against byte-identical local copies before commit.
inferred: Without corpus discovery, malformed or omitted historical records can escape compatibility review.
proposed: Effective-queue reconciliation should consume a commit-bound corpus report and stop whenever reconciliation_allowed is false.
superseded: Treating parser-readable records as the complete queue-update corpus without directory discovery.
unresolved: The live repository corpus has not yet been executed through this gate in CI.
A reviewed compatibility-exception manifest has not yet been committed for any actual legacy defects.

coverage: only explicit narrow reviewed exceptions permit compatibility

falsification_route: Add malformed JSON to a disposable fixture corpus and require blocking_parse_failure.
Add an uncovered defect and require blocking_incompatible.
Add an expired or incomplete exception and require blocking_invalid_exception.
Run against the full repository queue-update directory and compare discovered file count with an independent file listing.
